Document:
# Minor Crimes - These crimes are low severity, and can be instead punished with an NJP, or a warning.
Charge
Description
Punishment
Hooliganism
\- Behavior that is generally disruptive to the warship and crew that classifies as low-level shenanigans not deserving of more severe punishment. Things such as excessive window knocking, force-feeding other marines or failing to conduct oneself properly during the briefing, such as climbing the Briefing overview. This can escalate to [bold]Disorderly Conduct[/bold].
[bold]NJP.[/bold]
Disrespecting a Superior
\- Using offensive names or being directly disrespectful to someone of a higher rank or position.
[bold]NJP.[/bold]
False Testimony
\- To intentionally provide false information during a criminal investigation with the intent to disrupt said investigation.
[bold]5 minutes.[/bold]
Trespassing
\- Unauthorized access of an area which a person does not normally have access to or without command/superior approval.
[bold]Escort out of the area and optionally 7.5 minutes.[/bold]
Failure to Follow Procedure
\- Failing to follow the regulations found in the [bold]Standard Operating Procedure[/bold].
[bold]10 Minutes, equipment confiscation where relevant.[/bold]
Smoking in Medical Areas
\- Smoking in areas designated for use by medical personnel, such as the entire medbay and surgical rooms.
[bold]10 Minutes.[/bold]
Intoxication
\- During active duty, to consume alcohol or other substances such as hallucinogenic drugs that cause impairment of speech or mind of some form.
[bold]10 minutes, extended until the person is no longer impaired.[/bold]
Theft
\- To take items or property from another person or entity without their express permission. \n\- To retain possession of items that have been taken without permission or were knowingly not legal to be provided. A chance to return the item(s) or property should be given first. \n\- Removal of vendors from department areas without permission.
[bold]10 minutes, and the item(s) returned to the owner.[/bold]
